title: What the empty array in useEffect really means

options: Once, after the component first renders
After every render of the component
Before the component renders, so data is ready on first paint
mcq.explanation_md: The second argument is the dependency array — the list of values that should re-trigger the effect. An **empty** array says "this effect depends on nothing," so it runs once after the first render and never again. That's exactly what you want for load-on-mount fetching. Remove the array entirely and you get option B: the effect runs after *every* render — and since the fetch sets state, and setting state re-renders, you've built an infinite fetch loop. Option D is the other tempting one: effects always run *after* render, never before, which is why you need a loading state for that first data-less paint.
